sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.64 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: For finite groups$G$,$X$define$r(G;X)$to be the number of inequivalent actions of$G$on$X$, that is, the number of equivalence classes of homomorphisms$G \, X$, where equivalence is defined by conjugation by an element of$\,X$. Now define$r_G(n) := \{r(G;X) : |X| = n\}.$Is it true that$r_G(n)$may be bounded as a function of$(n)$, the total number (counting multiplicities) of prime factors of$n$?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
